title: ED raids five locations to probe Trinamool bank accounts, funds for chartered flights
content: “Searches are being conducted at five premises in the matter of alleged dishonest financial dealings, unlawful collection of money and routing of suspected funds through certain bank accounts of AITC,” says sources

title: Heavy rains bring relief to Delhi as Orange alerts issued across several States | Monsoon LIVE updates
content: Parts of southern Gujarat received “extremely heavy” rainfall, with the worst-affected being Surat district, where five persons died in rain-related incidents

title: Drass witnesses Shauryathon, donation of CT scan machine by Shiv Sena
content: Shiv Sena had donated a CT scan machine and an ambulance to the hospital, says Shiv Sena MP Shrikant Shinde

title: Wayanad debris slip: CM Satheesan holds emergency meeting; Agriculture Minister terms it man-made disaster
content: Agriculture Minister T. Siddique said the accident was caused by unscientifically stacked excavated soil, and alleged that the contractor’s failure to remove the accumulated soil on time led to the incident

title: After pro-separatist book fiasco, J&K L-G calls for examination of websites of J&K varsities, colleges for anti-India content
content: The L-G’s move came days after a book, titled ‘Great Personalities and Legends of J&K’, profiled Kashmir separatist leaders

title: Traffic bottleneck outside Sarath City mall leaves motorists stranded for hours on Gachibowli–Miyapur corridor; underpass, skywalk pitched
content: With up to 2.5 lakh weekend visitors, the mall frontage has become one of Hyderabad’s worst traffic hotspots as vehicles on six-lane squeeze into two-lane road. Inadequate pick-up and drop-off space cripple movement.

title: NCERT publishes revised Class 8 Social Science textbook, rewrites controversial chapter on judiciary
content: The controversial portions along with references to judicial backlog and two major court verdicts have been dropped; the revised textbook states in its acknowledgements that it has been published pursuant to the review process undertaken by complying with the Supreme Court’s directions

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: West Bengal Police intensify probe; examine shack as possible crime scene
content: “Three arrested accused were being questioned even as investigators were awaiting the forensic and viscera examination reports,” says senior police officer

title: Record 1.3 lakh pilgrims visit Amarnath shrine in 5 days in Kashmir
content: Our goal is to make the pilgrimage a truly memorable experience for everyone and they have access to the best possible amenities, says Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha

title: Ahead of Delhi protest, NC leaders meet civil society, pass resolution on Statehood for J&K
content: National Conference leaders term the delay in restoration of Statehood as ‘inordinate’ and describe the Centre’s ‘appropriate time phrase’ as a ‘vague and timeless expression’

title: Chief Minister Vijay “blackmailed” children by appealing to them, two election petitioners allege before Madras High Court
content: Justice V. Lakshminarayanan orders notices on the election petitions filed by DMK’s R.D. Shekar and S. Inigo Irudayaraj who had lost in Perambur and Tiruchi East constituencies

title: Staff stole cash, security protocols not enforced: Ram Temple SIT report
content: CCTV footage shows some counting staff hiding currency notes in clothes, pockets, and shoes; SIT identified procedural flaws in management of donations, to conduct re-audit of accounts

title: Decision over alliance with TVK yet to be reached, says Thirumavalavan
content: On July 1, the Chief Minister spoke to the parties that extended support, but no final decision was taken, he clarifies

title: Arcot MLA clarifies his absence at AIADMK consultative meeting
content: The Arcot MLA said that he had informed the party leadership about his inability to attend the consultative meeting at the AIADMK’s headquarters in Chennai

title: Cracks wide open in Punjab Congress as Channi camp skips Baghel’s outreach meetings
content: Punjab in-charge meets District Congress Committee chiefs, senior leaders as part of preparations for Assembly election; Warring downplays crisis, says Channi to meet Baghel within a day or two

title: 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts: High Court upholds death penalty for 38; government calls verdict a historic victory
content: On July 26, 2008, a series of 21 bomb blasts ripped through Ahmedabad in a span of 70 minutes, killing 56 persons and injuring 200 others

title: Residents oppose road cutting to lay new water pipelines in Tirupattur
content: Work to lay new water pipelines was proposed at a cost of ₹4.75 crore by the civic body a few months ago

title: Gang held for possession of ganja near Katpadi
content: The police said that as part of surveillance, they were patrolling the check post near Katpadi when the team intercepted the gang

title: Bengaluru firm builds a platform on which users can build their family trees up to eight generations; digitise family heirlooms
content: Its existing algorithms have the capacity to trace and store ancestral roots seven or up to eight generations

title: Residents protest operation of stone quarry near Vandavasi
content: Residents said that the existing stone quarry has polluted water bodies and groundwater, affecting agriculture in surrounding villages

title: CM invites KCR for debate on Kaleshwaram lapses in Assembly or joint sitting of Legislature
content: Revanth Reddy assures steps to see that KCR is not belittled; dates and duration of the session to be of the former Chief Minister’s choice

title: The little songbooks that chronicled a historic temple entry
content: Songbooks were published both in support of, and in opposition to, people belonging to the oppressed castes entering the Madurai Meenakshi Amman Temple for the first time, over 87 years ago

title: Panels will be set up for better coordination between party and Karnataka govt.: KPCC
content: Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee president B.K. Hariprasad said that for greater coordination between the State government and the party, a political committee and an implementation committee will be set up

title: Karnataka High Court questions why MLA M.C Sudhakar is not made accused in alleged land grab case against his father Chowdareddy, former Home Minister
content: The High Court of Karnataka has questioned why Chintamani MLA and former Higher Education Minister M.C. Sudhakar and his brother, M.C. Balaji, were not arraigned as accused in an alleged land grab case against their father, former Home Minister Chowdareddy, despite being named as direct beneficiaries of the encroached government land in the complaint

title: Karnataka Minister Khandre accuses Union government of selling HMT land for real estate businesses
content: Hitting back at Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy on the HMT forest land issue, Rural Development and Panchayat Raj Minister Eshwar Khandre on Tuessday accused the Centre of selling forest land illegally for real estate businesses

title: Online registration of select documents to be launched in Tamil Nadu on August 17: Minister
content: The documents include first sale of plots (by plot promoters) and the first sale of flats (by builders).

title: Senthilbalaji seeks anticipatory bail in ₹35-crore MLA bribery case
content: His plea is expected to be listed for hearing before Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan of Madras High Court on Wednesday

title: India supports two-state solution for solving Israel-Palestine conflict, says PM Modi
content: In a press statement in Jakarta while presiding over the signing of multiple agreements including the delivery of BrahMos supersonic cruise missile system and Astra Mk-1 beyond the visual-range air-to-air missiles to Indonesia, Mr. Modi says India believes that the role of dialogue and diplomacy has become more important than ever before

title: Wayanad debris slip updates: At least three dead, five others missing; region braces for more rains
content: The government has shifted local residents to safer locations. It has also halted traffic on the Meenakshi bridge and ordered the PWD to review its structural safety

title: Scientists develop health tracker for high-altitude yaks
content: The Internet of Things-based device is designed to address major challenges of yak management in remote regions and keep tabs on their movement near international borders in the Himalayas

title: ‘Flagging of labour rule violations could have led to CorroHealth lay-offs in Kozhikode’
content: The laid off staff are pinning their hopes on the talks scheduled between government officials and management representatives in Kochi on July 10

title: Monsoon tracker highlights: Wayanad on red alert after three dead in debris slip; Mumbai gets respite after two days
content: The alert was issued at 12.30 p.m. after the landslip at the Kalladi tunnel project site in Wayanad at around 11 a.m. Meanwhile, officials said ten people had died in rain-related incidents across Maharashtra's Palghar since July 1

title: Now, eggs are hurled at CPI(M) leader Minakshi Mukherjee
content: Since they were voted out of power on May 4, Trinamool leaders, including general secretary Abhishek Banerjee, MP Mahua Moitra, MLAs Madan Mitra and Kunal Ghosh, and several others, have had eggs hurled; this is the first such incident against a CPI(M) leader

title: BRICS nations adopt Guwahati Declaration to combat drug trafficking
content: India calls for a partnership built on speed, trust, and seamless real-time intelligence sharing to fight transnational drug syndicates

title: Four States reach agreement on Narmada project
content: Under the pact, the payments to be made towards the final settlement of pending dues have been resolved through a one-time settlement

title: 55% teachers are women, girl student enrolment up: govt.
content: UDISE+ report shows falling dropout rates and higher student retention; more schools get access to computers and internet; number of teachers up by 8.3% compared to 2022-23; learner progression across stages grows stronger

title: Karnataka SIR: Enumeration form distribution crosses 77% in Karnataka
content: Among districts, Belagavi recorded the highest number of enumeration forms distributed at 30.9 lakh, followed by Mysuru (22.27 lakh), Kalaburagi (19.07 lakh), Tumakuru (19.39 lakh) and Bengaluru Urban (16.9 lakh)

title: Why China's first submarine missile test in 43 years is ringing nuclear alarm bells
content: China launched a ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine into the Pacific Ocean on Monday, marking its first known submarine-launched ballistic missile test since 1982. The launch showcased the operational readiness of China's sea-based nuclear force, which forms one leg of the country's nuclear triad alongside land-based missiles and air-delivered nuclear weapons.

title: 'Life was enough': Student's final text to boyfriend before body found in Bengaluru lake
content: A 20-year-old undergraduate student was discovered deceased in Sankey Tank on Tuesday morning. Police suspect suicide after the student sent a message indicating intent to end her life. She reportedly entered the lake on Monday evening after leaving college and spending time nearby. Her body was found Tuesday morning after an extensive search by authorities and family. An investigation into the unnatural death is currently underway from all angles.

title: Explained: The VAR decision that left Egypt furious against Argentina
content: Argentina secured a dramatic 3-2 victory against Egypt in the World Cup Round of 16.  Egypt's camp expressed strong dissatisfaction with several officiating decisions during the match.  A disallowed Egyptian goal and the build-up to Argentina's winner fueled their anger.  Coach Hossam Hassan stated his team suffered clear injustice and unfair treatment.  Argentina advanced to the quarter-finals after this controversial and exciting encounter.

title: China may be planning to tell America: We do not need your Nvidia chips
content: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek is developing its own inference chips. This move aims to lessen dependence on Nvidia's hardware. US export controls have already impacted Nvidia's sales in China. Huawei is also a significant domestic competitor in this space. DeepSeek joins other AI developers creating in-house chip solutions.

title: 'Tehran's actions unacceptable': US revokes license for Iranian oil sale
content: The United States revoked an Iran oil sanctions waiver after recent tanker attacks. Three tankers were struck by projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz. One vessel caught fire off the coast of Oman following the incident. The Iranian foreign ministry condemned the US move as a breach of commitment. These events threaten to disrupt vital shipping through the strategic waterway.

title: What did Hassan’s furious ‘X’ gesture mean? Egypt boss’ yellow card moment explained
content: Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan sparked controversy during Egypt’s dramatic 3-2 FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 defeat to Argentina after making an “X” gesture toward referee François Letexier. While the gesture is recognised by FIFA as a signal against racist abuse, its meaning in this context remains unclear. FIFA has not confirmed any investigation, though Hassan’s actions may face disciplinary review.

title: Annual pain: Season’s 1st heavy rain brings Delhi-NCR to its knees
content: Delhi received its most intense monsoon rains since July second on Tuesday. Waterlogging and uprooted trees caused significant traffic disruptions across the city. Flight operations were also impacted, with over 300 flights experiencing delays at IGI Airport. The minimum temperature rose to thirty degrees Celsius, marking the warmest July night in two years. Air quality deteriorated to moderate levels but is expected to improve soon.

title: Virginia county manager: Electricity rates to rise sharply from July 1
content: As the tech landscape evolves with AI data centers, electricity costs are soaring. Henrico County, Virginia, has announced a staggering 25% hike in electricity bills for government facilities, prompting local officials to urge power conservation among their employees. Residents are also feeling the pinch, with some facing bill doubles. This phenomenon underscores the mounting pressure on community resources amid the rapid expansion of data centers.

title: FIFA launches racism probe after IShowSpeed abuse allegation during Argentina match
content: FIFA is investigating content creator IShowSpeed for alleged racist abuse.  This incident occurred during Argentina's World Cup match against Cape Verde.  Argentina secured a dramatic extra-time victory over Egypt in another thrilling game.  The defending champions came back from two goals down to win.  Argentina will now face Switzerland in the quarter-finals of the tournament.

title: Scientists thickened Arctic ice with seawater, but there's a catch
content: Scientists tested spraying seawater onto Arctic ice, which showed promising initial results. The treated ice became thicker and more reflective, resisting summer melting effectively. This method offers an alternative to controversial geoengineering approaches for ice preservation. While promising, researchers note significant challenges for large-scale implementation across the Arctic. Further work is essential before this technique can be considered for broader application.

title: Why warm and helpful people may have fewer friends, according to psychology
content: Helping friends constantly, from airport pick-ups to providing emotional support, may be a cover for deep-rooted insecurities. Psychologists note this 'caretaker' behavior often arises from an association of worth with usefulness, acting as a defense to shield oneself from vulnerability. This dynamic can create superficial relationships where the helpers feel unappreciated and overly exhausted, ultimately leading to isolation despite their admirable generosity.

title: Microsoft Xbox layoffs: CEO says 'We lost 64 cents on every dollar'
content: Microsoft's Xbox division is eliminating approximately 3,200 jobs as part of a major business restructure.  The company is resetting its strategy after experiencing slower-than-expected growth and rising costs.  Xbox is also simplifying its management structure, reducing layers from fourteen down to five.  Four studios, including Compulsion Games, will move to new independent ownership.  Xbox expects to return to growth in 2027 and entertain over a billion people daily.

title: Nolan Wells update: Family seeks answers after Horn Island trip
content: Nolan Xavier Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi student-athlete, was found dead on Horn Island after going missing during a Fourth of July trip with friends. Authorities have not announced a cause of death and say the investigation is ongoing. His family has hired attorney Ben Crump and is calling for a thorough, transparent review of the circumstances surrounding his death.

title: 'Dropped 35,000 feet in 2 minutes': Plane with 5 on board goes missing off Karachi
content: The missing aircraft, a Boeing 737 operated by Karachi-based K2 Airways, was flying from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates to Karachi when it reported a navigation system malfunction at around 9.18 pm local time, according to Pakistan's airport authority. Officials said radar data showed the aircraft suddenly descending and making a sharp change in direction at around 9.21 pm. Moments later, both radar and radio contact were lost about 155 nautical miles west of Karachi.

title: Lionel Messi, FIFA face bias claims after Argentina's win over Egypt
content: Argentina’s 3-2 victory over Egypt generated debate far beyond the final score. Lionel Messi’s missed penalty, controversial decisions and Gianni Infantino’s reaction from the stands became major talking points online. While some fans claimed Argentina received favorable treatment, no evidence supports those allegations. On the pitch, the defending champions recovered from adversity and earned another place in the World Cup quarter-finals.

title: Is Connor Murphy dead? Truth behind viral death rumours
content: Connor Murphy’s reported death remains an unconfirmed rumor as of July 2026. Claims circulating online largely stem from social media posts and statements that have not been independently verified. No family member, representative, or authority has confirmed his death. Murphy rose to fame through fitness and bodybuilding content, and questions about his current status remain unanswered pending official information.

title: Candace Owens, Andrew Kolvet clash over charlie Kirk's legacy
content: Candace Owens and Andrew Kolvet are locked in a public feud over Charlie Kirk’s legacy and the nature of Owens’ friendship with the late TPUSA founder. After Kolvet suggested Kirk was merely “placating” her, Owens responded with a lengthy X post, calling him a “CLOWN” and a liar. The dispute has widened existing divisions within conservative circles.

title: Wimbledon: Djokovic survives longest quarter-final ever, sets up Sinner showdown
content: Novak Djokovic battled past Felix Auger-Aliassime in a record-breaking Wimbledon quarter-final.  The Serbian star secured a thrilling five-set victory after more than five hours of play.  Djokovic will now face defending champion Jannik Sinner in the semi-finals.  Sinner advanced easily after defeating Jan-Lennard Struff in his quarter-final match.  This highly anticipated rematch promises another compelling tennis contest.

title: SIT names Avinash Shukla as accused No. 1 in Ayodhya Ram temple theft
content: A preliminary report indicates alleged thefts from Ram Temple donations over forty days. Avinash Shukla is identified as the central figure in this alleged embezzlement racket. Evidence includes CCTV footage and bank account analysis linking him to the crimes. Five other individuals are also named as accused in the ongoing investigation. Further inquiries are continuing into administrative accountability and institutional lapses.

title: 'The tournament is fixed': Egypt star alleges 'injustice' after Argentina defeat
content: Egypt's Mostafa Ziko accused officials of unfair play after their World Cup loss.  He claimed the tournament was fixed following a dramatic Argentine comeback.  Egypt led two-nil before Argentina scored three late goals.  A disallowed Ziko goal early in the second half proved pivotal.  The forward apologized to fans, stating players gave their best effort.

title: 'They wanted Messi to stay': Egypt coach accuses officials of favouring Argentina
content: Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan accused FIFA and match officials of treating his side "unfairly" after their dramatic 3-2 FIFA World Cup Round of 16 defeat to Argentina. Furious over key refereeing decisions, Hassan suggested external influences helped keep Lionel Messi and the defending champions in the tournament, insisting Egypt had been "cheated" after surrendering a two-goal lead.

title: Secret wedding, chilling chats, Snapchat clue: What we know about Ketan Agarwal murder case
content: Pune police are investigating realtor Ketan Agarwal's murder, focusing on his fiancée Siya Goyal.  Investigators are examining evidence suggesting Goyal secretly married her alleged lover, Chetan Chaudhary.  The accused allegedly pushed Agarwal off a cliff at Lohagad Fort on June 18.  Digital evidence, including chat records, is under scrutiny for further clues.  The investigation also considers Goyal's Snapchat message hinting at a canceled wedding.

title: Kerala govt blames to 'unscientific dumping' after Wayanad landslip kills 3
content: A massive landslip at a Kerala tunnel construction site killed three workers. Five other migrant laborers remain missing after the devastating mudslide occurred. The Kerala government described the incident as a man-made disaster. Unscientific dumping of excavated soil was blamed for the catastrophic event. Rescue operations are underway to find the missing individuals.

title: Crude loses cool: Oil prices soar 3% as Middle East tensions flare again
content: Oil prices rose sharply after United States military strikes targeted Iran. These actions followed attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Qatar blamed Iran for striking a liquefied natural gas tanker and an oil supertanker. Maritime security warned of severe threats to ships crossing the Strait. This has reignited fears of further disruptions to vital oil transit routes.

title: 'Orwellian': UK plan to force YouTube to prioritise BBC over independent creators draws ire
content: UK government plans may force social media platforms to prioritize state news.  This move has drawn accusations of state censorship and Orwellian control.  Critics argue it will suppress independent creators and limit audience discovery.  YouTube has warned creators about potential impacts on their visibility and revenue.  The proposals aim to combat misinformation by controlling content visibility online.

title: 'He can still make a comeback': Gambhir breaks silence on Samson snub
content: Gautam Gambhir clarified Sanju Samson's position within the team's plans.  He stated that player discussions remain strictly private between them.  Current player form influences selection decisions for the national side.  International cricket demands that players consistently earn their places.  India suffered a record defeat against England in the third T20I.

title: 'Iran violated truce': US launches 'punishment' strikes after attacks on 3 tankers in Hormuz
content: United States forces launched strikes against Iranian targets early Wednesday. These actions followed attacks on three merchant ships in waters off Oman. Iran stated it would take necessary measures to safeguard its national security. The United States had also revoked a license authorizing Iranian oil sales. This revocation occurred hours after the tankers were struck by projectiles.

title: After govt notice, Meta details child safety steps
content: Meta revealed new child safety measures after an Indian government notice regarding Instagram ads.  The company disabled numerous offending accounts and advertisements using advanced artificial intelligence tools.  Meta stated its systems detect suspicious off-platform links and other exploitation signals.  Globally, over four million accounts were removed last year for suspicious child-related activity.  The company continues to invest in technology and work with law enforcement agencies.

title: 'It got Rs 160 crore from TMC’: ED searches Kolkata company over aircraft deal
content: Enforcement Directorate searched five locations in Kolkata Tuesday.  The agency is probing an aviation firm's alleged financial dealings.  Carewell Aviation reportedly received Rs 160 crore from TMC funds.  This money was allegedly used to purchase an aircraft and a helicopter.  The ED is investigating the unlawful collection and routing of these suspected funds.

title: MS Dhoni turns 45, steals the show at Trent Bridge during India vs England 3rd T20I
content: Former India captain MS Dhoni celebrated his 45th birthday at Trent Bridge. He was seen in the hospitality stands during the India versus England T20I match. Broadcasters showed the legendary wicketkeeper-batter on the big screen, drawing a large crowd cheer. Sunil Gavaskar wished Dhoni a happy birthday and commented on his youthful appearance. Dhoni's presence at the match quickly went viral across social media platforms.

title: 4 officials from environment minister Bhupender Yadav's office removed
content: Four officials from Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav's office were removed on July 3.  Amar Singh, a private secretary, was removed on administrative grounds.  Political appointees Siddharth Yadav and Ayush Saran had their appointments terminated.  Shailesh Kumar Singh was prematurely repatriated to his parent cadre.  The ministry has not provided specific reasons for these abrupt removals.

title: 'Alpha' records good growth on Tuesday, crosses Rs 70 crore worldwide
content: Alpha's box office numbers saw good growth on its fifth day. Strategic ticket prices helped boost collections and audience footfalls significantly. The film earned an estimated Rs 4.25 crore net on Tuesday. Its India gross collection has reached an estimated Rs 50.23 crore. The movie aims to sustain momentum and potentially cross Rs 100 crore.

title: The psychology of luck: Why some people seem to get lucky more often
content: Psychologist Richard Wiseman's research suggests luck is not random fate.  Lucky people actively create and spot opportunities around them.  They trust their intuition and maintain optimistic expectations for good outcomes.  These individuals also learn from setbacks, turning bad luck into valuable lessons.  Wiseman's "Luck School" demonstrated that these habits can indeed be taught.

title: Quote of the day by Leonardo DiCaprio: 'Life is a gift and I don't intend on wasting it
content: Leonardo DiCaprio's career spans decades, showcasing his versatile acting talent. He has starred in numerous acclaimed films across various genres. The actor champions environmental causes and wildlife conservation efforts. His life philosophy emphasizes living in the moment and embracing opportunities. DiCaprio's journey reflects a dedication to his craft and a grateful outlook.

title: Verifying if Siya was married to Chetan: Police
content: Police are verifying claims of marriage between Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, accused in Ketan Agarwal's murder. Cellphone data revealed Siya Goyal researched a similar murder case online before the incident. The couple claimed a secret marriage occurred a few months prior to the investigation. Ketan Agarwal died after being pushed off a cliff at Lohagad on June 18.

title: Gold Jumped 245% In 4 Years: Should You Buy More, Hold Or Book Profits?
content: Growth-oriented investors with a long investment horizon and significant equity exposure may need only 5-10 per cent of their portfolio in gold.

title: Arvind Kejriwal's Ex-Home, Dubbed 'Sheesh Mahal' By BJP, To Be A Guest House
content: The bungalow was often dubbed as 'sheesh mahal' by the BJP to attack Kejriwal with allegations of corruption.

title: Choosing A WhatsApp Username? Expert Warns Of 6 Cybersecurity Risks
content: India recorded 24.65 lakh cyber fraud complaints. AI scams, digital arrests, fake investment schemes and identity theft are already rising rapidly.

title: Mexican Man Shot Dead By ICE Agent After Allegedly Trying To Run Over Officer
content: A US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent fatally shot a motorist in Houston on Tuesday while officers were trying to stop the man's vehicle, the agency said in a statement, in the midst of an escalating federal crackdown on migrants.

title: Delhi Revives Child Rights Commission After 3 Years; Watchdog Gets New Team
content: The Delhi government on Tuesday reconstituted the Delhi Commission for Protection of Child Rights (DCPCR), appointing a Chairperson and four members to restore the functioning of the statutory body.

title: Amit Shah, Rekha Gupta Launch Delhi's 70-Lakh Tree Drive, Kick Off Ridge Revival
content: Amit Shah said nearly 5,000 hectares of the Ridge had now been brought under legal forest protection after the final notification remained pending for almost three decades.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Heavy Rain Causes Traffic Jams, Waterlogging In Delhi
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Delhi witnesses its wettest day with heavy rains causing waterlogged roads and traffic congestion.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US Launches Fresh Strikes Against Iran, Revokes Waiver After Hormuz Attacks
content: Iran War: The United States launched "power strikes" against Iran following a series of attacks by Tehran on commercial ships near the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Global Crackdown On Lawrence Bishnoi Gang: 24 Arrested, Assassination Plot Busted
content: According to federal prosecutors, Bishnoi allegedly directed an international criminal network from inside prison using contraband mobile phones and encrypted internet communication devices.

title: On Camera, Israeli Officer Tosses Stun Grenade Into Car Carrying Palestinians
content: The CCTV clip, recorded on Sunday and released by Israeli human rights group B'Tselem, shows an officer confronting a vehicle before the situation quickly escalates.

title: Himanta Sarma Backs Tough Anti-Drug Policing, Promises Stricter Action In Assam
content: Assam chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma defended his government's crackdown on narcotics, saying the fight against the drug menace requires firm policing, coordinated action across state.

title: Prostitution Racket Busted In Bihar; 18 Detained, Including 4 Minors
content: Police carried out a major raid against an alleged prostitution racket in the Bharatiya Nagar area of Saharsa district on Tuesday, detaining 18 people, including four minor girls.

title: Iran Warns Of "Decisive" Action After US Strikes Around Strait Of Hormuz
content: Iranian state media on Tuesday reported a series of explosions heard in the Strait of Hormuz region, moments after US officials announced "powerful" strikes on Iran.

title: At NATO Summit, Trump Reiterates US Should Control Greenland
content: Donald Trump said Greenland should be controlled by the United States, not Denmark, reaffirming a stance that has caused tensions among NATO allies just as leaders of the alliance gathered for a summit in Turkey.

title: US Launches "Series Of Powerful Strikes" On Iran After Hormuz Attacks
content: The strikes came just hours after the three tankers were hit by projectiles while sailing through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Revokes Iran Oil Waiver After Tanker Attacks In Strait Of Hormuz
content: The US Treasury Department canceled a license announced in June that had allowed Iran to produce, sell and deliver crude oil and related products through August 21.

title: French Far-Right Leader Le Pen Says She Will Run For President Next Year
content: Born in 1968, Le Pen was just four years old when her father Jean-Marie co-founded the National Front (FN), France's main postwar far-right movement.

title: Rape-Murder Of 11-Year-Old In Bengal Pre-Planned: Police After Three Arrests
content: At the crime scene, NDTV found that to avoid public attention, the criminals had chosen a deserted area adjacent to a railway track near the Suryapur station.

title: Air India, AI Express Flights Come Face-To-Face On Mumbai Runway
content: The incident took place at around 10 pm. The Air India Express plane was yet to vacate the runway after landing when the Delhi-bound Air India aircraft was preparing to take off from the same runway, the sources said.

title: Erling Haaland loves this high-protein and prebiotic-rich breakfast; detailed recipe inside
content: The TOI Lifestyle Desk is a dynamic team of dedicated journalists who, with unwavering passion and commitment, sift through the pulse of the nation to curate a vibrant tapestry of lifestyle news for The Times of India readers. title: Govt urges USTR to review its move to levy new tariffs
content: the move does not meet legal standards, lacks country-specific analysis, govt says . the move fails to establish causal link between absence of import prohibitions and its impact of US business . several Indian companies have petitioned against the move seen as a replacement for Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs .

title: 9 Indian ships with over 190 seafarers still near Hormuz
content: at present, only nine Indian vessels are in the Persian Gulf region - in the west of Strait of Hormuz . there are no updates on whether ships from India are waiting to go to West Asia through the Strait to pick up fresh consignments . industry insiders say it may take a little longer for shipping lines to plan resume sending their vessels to the area .

title: Landslides disrupt Maharashtra traffic, Mumbai-Pune rail services remain off track
content: Neha Madaan is a senior feature writer at The Times of India, Pune. She holds an M A degree in Mass Communication and Journalism from University of Pune. She covers tourism, heritage development and its conservation, apart from an array of subjects such as civic issues, environment, astronomy, civic school education as well as social issues concerning persons with disabilities. Her interests include metaphysical research and animal rights. Read More

title: After SC rap, NCERT rewrites Class VIII social science chapter on judiciary
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More
